title: 10 best herbal creams for bloating

remedy_properties: Irritant properties: The menthol content in peppermint cream stimulates digestion and relieves bloating by irritating the digestive tract, which helps to stimulate the movement of food through the system.
Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint's antispasmodic compounds help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to bloating.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in peppermint cream have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with bloating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with bloating because of its natural digestive properties. The menthol and menthone in peppermint oil stimulate the muscles in the stomach and intestines, helping to ease gas and reduce discomfort. Additionally, peppermint creams can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing food to pass through more efficiently. This can lead to a significant reduction in bloating and discomfort, making peppermint creams a popular natural remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol acts as a natural carminative and antispasmodic, helping to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate cramps associated with bloating.
Piperitenone: Piperitenone has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to bloating and discomfort.

Royleanone: Royleanone, a labdane diterpene, exhibits anti-spasmodic and carminative properties, which can help ease muscle contractions and gas release in the digestive system, providing relief from bloating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Peppermint leaves contain menthol, a natural compound that helps to relax the muscles and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
Leaves: The leaves also contain menthone, which has a calming effect on the stomach and can help alleviate symptoms of bloating and discomfort.
Leaves: In addition, the leaves contain other compounds like limonene and beta-pinene, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help to soothe digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves from a clean and pesticide-free area.
Steam distill the harvested leaves for 2 hours to obtain 50g of essential oil.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 10g of candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Combine 30g of mentha x piperita essential oil with 50g of coconut oil and 20g of shea butter.
Whisk the mixture thoroughly for 5 minutes until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Digestive Enzyme Stimulation: Herbal ginger creams contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which stimulate digestive enzymes to break down proteins and carbohydrates more efficiently, reducing bloating and discomfort.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Ginger has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of bloating, cramps, and discomfort.

Gastric Motility Regulation: The bioactive compounds in herbal ginger creams can regulate gastric motility, promoting regular bowel movements and preventing stagnation or backup of food and gas in the intestines, which can contribute to bloating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with bloating because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, gingerol, relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with bloating. Additionally, ginger has been shown to improve digestion and reduce gas production in the gut, making it an effective remedy for alleviating bloating and discomfort. This natural remedy promotes a feeling of relief and comfort, allowing individuals to manage their symptoms and maintain a healthy digestive system.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpene compounds have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce bloating.

Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols are also sesquiterpene compounds that have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic effects, which can help to alleviate bloating and discomfort in the abdominal region.

Volatile oils (specifically, zingiberene and bisabolol): These terpene compounds have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to reduce inflammation and gas in the digestive system, thereby alleviating bloating.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale in skincare products, due to its high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Used to create creams that help soothe digestive issues and reduce bloating, thanks to their ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract.
Buds: Employed in creams to combat bloating and inflammation, as they are rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry 250 grams of fresh zingiber officinale rhizome for future use in the recipe.
Combine 50 grams of dried zingiber officinale with 100 grams of beeswax and 200 grams of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 20 minutes.
Add 10 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir thoroughly for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel cream helps to reduce bloating by releasing trapped gas in the digestive system, relieving pressure and discomfort.
Antispasmodic properties: Fennel's antispasmodic compounds relax the muscles of the digestive tract, reducing cramping, spasms, and inflammation that can contribute to bloating.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate bloating and discomfort caused by inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with bloating because of its carminative properties, which aid in the release of trapped gas in the digestive system. The active compounds in fennel, particularly anethole, have been shown to relax the muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with bloating. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe and calm the digestive system, further alleviating symptoms of bloating and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: A terpene found in Foeniculum vulgare, fenchone has anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve gas and bloating in the digestive system.

Anethole: A phenolic compound present in Foeniculum vulgare, anethole has a mild anti-inflammatory effect and can help to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, thereby reducing spasms and alleviating bloating and discomfort.
Fenchyl acetate: A terpene found in Foeniculum vulgare, fenchyl acetate has a soothing effect on the digestive system and can help to reduce inflammation and relieve gas and bloating.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly fennel seed oil, which has natural carminative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to create creams that ease bloating and discomfort due to their content of volatile oils, which help to relax the digestive system and reduce gas.
Stems: The stems of Foeniculum vulgare are also used to make creams for bloating relief, as they contain a mixture of essential oils and antioxidants that promote digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves in late summer when they are most potent and fragrant.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve them.
Infuse 1 cup of dried foeniculum vulgare in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and combine it with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools to create a smooth and fragrant herbal cream.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of bloating such as abdominal discomfort.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice creams have been shown to have a soothing effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and improving digestion, which can help alleviate bloating and other digestive issues.

Isomaltose: This natural sweetener has prebiotic properties, meaning it helps feed beneficial gut bacteria, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and reducing symptoms of bloating such as gas and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with bloating because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin that helps with bloating by inhibiting the activity of the enzyme 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which converts cortisol into a more active form, thereby reducing fluid retention and alleviating bloating symptoms.

Liquiritin: A phenolic glycoside that exhibits anti-inflammatory properties and helps with bloating by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, which in turn decreases the production of gas and alleviates discomfort.

remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and carminative properties.
Leaves: They are used to add a soothing and calming effect to creams, helping to alleviate bloating and digestive discomfort.
Buds: They are used to provide additional anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits, which can help reduce inflammation and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier and clean them thoroughly.
Combine 200 grams of the dried roots with 1000 ml of distilled water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes then let it steep for 1 hour.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a separate container and discard the solids.
Mix 100 grams of the herbal liquid with 200 grams of emulsifying wax and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: The primary active compound in turmeric, curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with bloating.
Gingerol: A bioactive compound found in ginger, gingerol has natural carminative properties that can help ease digestion, reduce flatulence, and alleviate symptoms of bloating.

Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are also widely used in creams for bloating as they contain a significant amount of curcumin, which helps to alleviate digestive discomfort.
Stems: Some creams for bloating may use the stems of Curcuma longa, which contain smaller amounts of curcumin, but still contribute to the overall anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried curcuma longa root from a reputable source.
Infuse 1 cup of carrier oil with 2 tablespoons of dried curcuma longa root for 2 weeks.
Combine 1/2 cup of the infused carrier oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10-15 minutes or until fully melted.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers on a dry sunny day for maximum potency.

Infuse 10 grams of dried taraxacum officinale in 100ml of carrier oil like jojoba oil for 2 weeks.

remedy_recipe: Combine 200 grams of citrus x aurantium peel with 200 grams of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60-70 degrees celsius for 2 hours then filter it through a cheesecloth.
Combine 100 grams of herbal extract with 500 grams of distilled water and 200 grams of glycerin in a bowl.
Mix the ingredients well and heat the mixture at 50-60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes then cool it down.
Add 100 grams of beeswax to 500 grams of cooled mixture and melt it in a double boiler.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 ginkgo biloba leaves from a clean environment to avoid contamination.
Extract the essential oil from the ginkgo biloba leaves by using a solvent like glycerin at a 1:10 ratio for 2 weeks.
Combine 200 grams of beeswax with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt.
Mix 10 grams of ginkgo biloba extract with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before cutting into bars.

Combine dried salvia officinalis leaves with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Infuse the mixture for 2 weeks shaking the container daily and then strain the liquid.
Mix the infused liquid with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 milliliters of coconut oil then heat.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the eucalyptus powder with 50 milliliters of coconut oil in a small bowl, stirring well for 3 minutes to ensure even distribution.
Add 20 grams of beeswax pellets to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until melted.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping with an electric whisk for 2-3 minutes until smooth and creamy consistency is achieved.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at a temperature below 4°c for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and rinse them with cool water.
Combine the leaves with 1/4 cup of organic coconut oil in a glass jar and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding solids.
Mix the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars filling them to the brim and allowing it to cool and set for 30 minutes.

Buds: High in flavonoids and phenolic acids, buds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate bloating symptoms like abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20g of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground cinnamomum verum with 10ml of sweet almond oil and 5ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to create a fragrant infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids, and let it cool to room temperature for 1 hour.
Whip the cooled oil with 10g of beeswax and 2ml of vitamin e oil in a mixing bowl until smooth and creamy, then fill sterilized containers and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage creams helps with bloating because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe digestive issues, reducing discomfort and tension in the abdominal area. The creams also promote healthy gut bacteria, which aids in proper digestion and absorption of nutrients, ultimately alleviating symptoms of bloating. Additionally, borage's omega-6 fatty acids help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, providing relief from bloating and discomfort associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive disorders.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Borage cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), which helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate bloating symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of borage cream help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to bloating and discomfort.

Prostaglandin-regulating properties: Borage cream contains fatty acid precursors that help regulate prostaglandins, hormone-like substances involved in inflammation and relaxation of smooth muscle in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate bloating and cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, borage roots help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with bloating.
Seeds: High in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a fatty acid that supports the body's natural anti-inflammatory response and alleviates symptoms of bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried flowers of borago officinalis and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned flowers with enough vodka to create a 1:4 ratio and steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a bowl, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Mix the extracted liquid with beeswax and coconut oil in a ratio of 1 part extract to 2 parts wax and oil.
Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the cream thickens.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with bloating because of its ability to relax the smooth muscle lining in the digestive tract. The menthol content in wintergreen helps to reduce spasms and cramps, allowing food to pass more smoothly through the system and relieving pressure on the digestive organs. Additionally, wintergreen's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe irritated tissues and reduce swelling, further contributing to a reduction in bloating and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol, a key component of wintergreen oil, helps to alleviate bloating by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms, and relieving cramping discomfort.

Gingerol: Gingerol, another active compound found in wintergreen, possesses anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with bloating, while also stimulating digestion and improving circulation.

Cannabinoids: Wintergreen creams often contain cannabinoids, such as CBD, which have been shown to help regulate the gut microbiome, reducing inflammation and promoting a balanced digestive system, thereby alleviating bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wintergreen roots contain a high concentration of methyl salicylate, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of bloating.
Barks: The bark of the wintergreen plant contains salicin, a natural analgesic that can help to soothe digestive discomfort and relieve bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and flowers.
Grind the herbal material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 6 tablespoons of coconut oil with 4 tablespoons of beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of ground gaultheria procumbens to the melted oil and wax mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the cream into a sterilized container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 60 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Juniper creams helps with bloating because they possess natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The active compounds in juniper, such as terpenes and flavonoids, have been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms that can lead to bloating. Additionally, juniper's antispasmodic properties help soothe the digestive system, easing cramps and discomfort associated with bloating. As a result, juniper creams can provide fast-acting relief from bloating symptoms, leaving the body feeling light, comfortable, and relaxed.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Juniper cream helps to ease bloating by reducing the formation of gas in the digestive tract, allowing for easier digestion and relief from discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in juniper cream reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive system, which can contribute to bloating and discomfort, helping to alleviate symptoms.
Digestive stimulant properties: Juniper cream stimulates digestive enzymes and improves gut motility, helping to speed up digestion and relieve constipation, a common cause of bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Juniper roots have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in soothing digestive discomfort and reducing bloating.
Barks: Juniper barks contain antioxidants and terpenes that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of bloating and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh juniperus communis leaves and 1 cup of dried leaves in a clean environment.
Combine the fresh and dried leaves in a large pot filled with 4 liters of distilled water, boil for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding solids after 60 minutes.
Add 1 cup of sweet almond oil and 1/2 cup of beeswax to the liquid, heating the mixture in a double boiler for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat, let cool and thicken for 30 minutes before whipping into a smooth cream consistency using an electric mixer for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew creams helps with bloating because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which ease digestive discomfort and reduce swelling in the abdomen. The herb's active compounds, such as parthenolide and sesquiterpene lactones, work to soothe irritated tissues and relax muscle contractions that can contribute to bloating. Digestive soothing agents: Feverfew creams contain soothing agents like flavonoids, which help calm irritations and reduce inflammation in the digestive system, promoting a healthy gut environment and alleviating symptoms of bloating.

Leaves: The leaves of the feverfew plant are rich in parthenolide, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort associated with bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tanacetum parthenium leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried and before noon sun exposure.
Dry the plant material at 40°c for 24 hours to prevent spoilage and preserve flavonoids and terpenes.
Crush 50g of dried tanacetum parthenium into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for efficient extraction.
Mix the powdered tanacetum parthenium with 200ml of olive oil and 10% glycerin at room temperature for 2 hours for optimal infusion.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer to sterilized containers, allowing it to mature for 14 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with bloating because they contain the natural anti-inflammatory and digestive properties of the yarrow plant. The cream's active ingredients help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with bloating. Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to bloating and discomfort. By decreasing inflammation, yarrow creams can help alleviate symptoms.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort associated with bloating.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of volatile oils, which help to calm digestive issues and reduce bloating symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers for infusion in 250ml of boiling water.
Steep the infused mixture for 15-20 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Mix the resulting liquid with 30g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil to create a cream base.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until ingredients are fully incorporated.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for bloating are topical products that contain natural ingredients such as peppermint, ginger, and fennel to help alleviate digestive discomfort. These creams work by relaxing the muscles in the stomach and reducing inflammation, providing quick relief from bloating. Examples of herbal creams that help with bloating include peppermint cream and ginger cream, which have been known to improve digestion and reduce discomfort, allowing individuals to live their lives more comfortably and confidently.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bloating that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil can soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. These creams can also help to reduce redness and itching associated with these conditions, promoting healthy and clear skin.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with bloating is a blend of peppermint, ginger, and chamomile. Peppermint cream can ease digestion and reduce inflammation, while ginger cream can alleviate cramps and nausea. Chamomile cream, rich in antioxidants, can soothe the stomach and promote relaxation. By applying these creams topically, you can target the root causes of bloating and experience relief from discomfort. This natural approach can help regulate your digestive system and promote overall well-being. Regular use can lead to long-term benefits.
